diagrams.title: Problem Decomposition in OOP
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing how a complex problem breaks down into smaller, manageable classes and methods, demonstrating the 'divide and conquer' strategy applied to object-oriented design.
elements: Main Problem (large rounded rectangle at top)
Arrow pointing downward to first-level decomposition
Two sub-problems (smaller rectangles branching left and right)
Arrow from each sub-problem to corresponding class boxes
Class boxes containing method icons inside
Connecting lines showing the decomposition hierarchy
diagrams.purpose: Explains how to break down a large programming problem into cohesive classes and methods, a fundamental OOP problem-solving strategy

diagrams.title: OOP Problem Solving Loop
diagrams.description: A circular process diagram showing the iterative cycle of understanding, planning, implementing, and testing in OOP problem solving.
elements: Four connected circular nodes in a clockwise loop
Node 1: 'Understand Problem' with question mark icon
Node 2: 'Design Classes' with class diagram icon
Node 3: 'Implement Code' with Python code brackets icon
Node 4: 'Test & Refactor' with checkmark icon
Arrows connecting each node showing circular flow
Central label: 'OOP Development Cycle'
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the iterative nature of problem solving in Python OOP, emphasizing that solutions are refined through multiple cycles

diagrams.title: Class Collaboration for Problem Solving
diagrams.description: A diagram showing how different classes collaborate and send messages to each other to solve a unified problem, demonstrating the strategy of leveraging existing components.
elements: Three class boxes arranged in a triangle formation
Class 1: 'Solver' at top with method signatures
Class 2: 'DataHandler' at bottom-left with attributes
Class 3: 'Validator' at bottom-right with validation logic
Dashed arrow lines connecting classes representing method calls
Message labels on arrows showing what each class provides
Central 'Problem Solution' label in overlapping area
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the OOP strategy of composing solutions from collaborating objects, showing how encapsulation and message passing solve problems
